title: 'No Kings' Protests Draw 3,100+ Events Nationwide on Iran War One-Month Mark
detail: On March 28, 2026 — coinciding with the one-month anniversary of the US-Israel strikes on Iran — more than 3,100 anti-Trump protests branded 'No Kings' took place across all 50 states, with organizers reporting millions of attendees. Protesters cited the Iran war, the rising cost of living driven by oil prices and tariffs, the ongoing DHS shutdown, and Trump's use of executive power. Two-thirds of RSVPs came from outside major urban centers, including hundreds of events in red-state suburban and rural areas — a notable geographic broadening compared to prior Trump-era demonstrations. Rallies featured signs referencing the ongoing Iran war, DOGE cuts to essential services, and TSA staffing chaos. The protests were among the largest domestic demonstrations of Trump's second term.

media.caption: No Kings protests draw thousands of events nationwide against Iran war and Trump policies

title: Iran War Hits One-Month Mark: Houthis Strike Israel; Iran Hits Saudi Base, Injuring 12 US Troops
detail: The US-Israel war on Iran reached its one-month mark on March 28, 2026, with two significant new escalations. Yemen's Houthi forces launched their first missile barrage of the entire conflict, targeting Israeli military sites; air raid sirens activated in Beersheba and eight impact sites were reported in Tel Aviv, killing at least one person and wounding several. Separately, Iran struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia — a shared US-Saudi facility near Riyadh — with ballistic missiles and drones, injuring 12 US service members (two seriously) in the first US-troop-casualty event at a Saudi base during this conflict. The one-month toll now stands at roughly 1,900+ Iranians killed and 13 US service members killed in theater. Trump maintained his April 6 deadline for Iranian energy-infrastructure strikes, citing ongoing indirect talks via Pakistan as justification for the continued pause. G7 Foreign Ministers, meeting in Cernay-la-Ville, France, identified the Iran war as the summit's primary agenda item. Secretary Rubio said 'some concrete progress has been made' in negotiations.

media.caption: Iran war hits one-month mark as Houthis strike Israel

title: House Passes GOP DHS Bill; Senate Deadlock Continues; Trump and Canada PM Carney Hold First Call
detail: House Republicans passed a 60-day DHS funding bill on March 28 that fully funds ICE and Border Patrol, rejecting the bipartisan Senate-passed bill from the prior night that excluded those immigration enforcement agencies as a Democratic condition. Senate Democrats immediately signaled the House bill was dead on arrival, leaving DHS in its longest funding lapse — now exceeding six weeks. Trump's executive order to restore TSA agent pay had provided some short-term relief but the structural deadlock remained unresolved, with 480+ officer resignations and hour-long security lines persisting at major airports. In a separate development, President Trump and newly installed Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney spoke by phone in what both described as 'cordial and extremely productive' — their first direct conversation since Carney took office. Carney warned of Canadian retaliatory tariffs if US reciprocal tariffs planned for April 2 take effect on Canadian goods; both leaders agreed to begin comprehensive negotiations on a new bilateral economic and security relationship following Canada's scheduled April 28 federal election. Canada had already imposed C$30 billion in retaliatory tariffs on US goods in response to earlier Trump tariffs on steel and aluminum.
